title: Supreme Court Affirms Right to Walk on Footpaths; Congress Demands Fundamental Right to Vote
summary: The Supreme Court of India passed an order recognising the right to walk on footpaths as a fundamental right, citing a surge in pedestrian deaths across India. The ruling drew attention to urban infrastructure failures and pedestrian safety. Following this, the Congress party demanded that the right to vote also be elevated to a fundamental right, linking the judicial reasoning to electoral rights discourse.
exam_angle: Tests understanding of judicial expansion of fundamental rights under Article 21 (Right to Life), and the distinction between constitutional rights and statutory rights in India.
key_facts: SC ruled right to walk on footpaths is a fundamental right
Basis: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Context: Rising pedestrian deaths in urban India
Congress sought similar fundamental-right status for the right to vote

title: Police Further Investigation After Final Report: Supreme Court Clarifies CrPC Provisions
summary: The Supreme Court addressed a significant procedural question: whether police require court permission to conduct further investigation after filing a final report (chargesheet) under the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC). The court clarified the scope of Sections 173(8) of CrPC, which allows further investigation even post-chargesheet. This has implications for criminal justice administration and protection of accused persons' rights.
exam_angle: Relevant for understanding criminal procedure law under CrPC Section 173(8), judicial oversight in criminal investigations, and rights of accused persons.
key_facts: Provision in question: Section 173(8) of CrPC (now BNSS equivalent)
Court examined whether prior court permission is mandatory for further investigation post-final report
Relates to balance between investigative powers and judicial oversight
Relevant for GS Paper 2 and law optional aspirants

title: India–US Interim Trade Deal Needs 'Final Touches'; USTR Greer to Visit New Delhi on June 23–24
summary: India and the United States are on the verge of finalising an interim bilateral trade deal, described as requiring only 'final touches'. US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer is scheduled to visit New Delhi on June 23–24, 2026, to conclude negotiations. The deal is part of broader efforts to manage tariff tensions between the two countries following US President Trump's tariff actions.
exam_angle: Important for India–US trade relations, the concept of interim/partial trade agreements, and India's strategy on preferential trade arrangements. Links to GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy).
key_facts: USTR Jamieson Greer to visit New Delhi: June 23–24, 2026
India–US interim trade deal described as needing only 'final touches'
Context: US tariff actions under Trump administration
India may seek a sunset clause in the pact (Business Standard)

title: India Seeks Sunset Clause in US Trade Pact Amid Tariff Concerns
summary: India is reportedly seeking the inclusion of a sunset clause in the proposed interim trade agreement with the United States, amid concerns over long-term tariff commitments. A sunset clause would allow the agreement to expire after a fixed period unless renewed, giving India flexibility to renegotiate terms. This reflects India's cautious approach to trade deals in a volatile global tariff environment.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of trade negotiation concepts such as sunset clauses, preferential tariff regimes, and India's trade policy strategy.
key_facts: Sunset clause: a provision causing a law/agreement to expire after a set period unless renewed
India's demand reflects concern over permanent tariff concessions to the US
Context: ongoing India–US trade negotiations under Trump tariff regime
Related: FTA wave may bring 60% of India's trade under preferential regime (Economic Times)

title: Mega FTA Wave: 60% of India's Trade to Come Under Preferential Regime
summary: According to a report by The Economic Times, India's ongoing and concluded Free Trade Agreement (FTA) negotiations could bring approximately 60% of the country's total trade under a preferential tariff regime. India has been actively pursuing FTAs with the EU, UK, US, and other trading partners. This would mark a significant shift from India's historically protectionist trade stance.
exam_angle: Connects to India's trade policy evolution, the significance of FTAs for economic integration, and debates on trade liberalisation vs. protectionism in GS Paper 3.
key_facts: 60% of India's trade may come under preferential tariff regime via FTAs
Key FTAs in progress: India–EU, India–UK, India–US (interim)
Preferential trade regime: lower/zero tariffs for partner countries
Signals shift from India's traditional protectionist trade policy

title: BRICS Energy Ministers' Meeting to be Hosted by India in Gurugram on June 25–26
summary: India will host the BRICS Energy Ministers' Meeting in Gurugram (Haryana) on June 25–26, 2026. The meeting will focus on energy security, clean energy transitions, and cooperation among BRICS nations. India holds the BRICS Presidency in 2026, making this a significant diplomatic and energy-policy event.
exam_angle: Relevant for India's BRICS Presidency 2026, multilateral energy diplomacy, and India's clean energy commitments. BRICS energy cooperation is an important IR topic.
key_facts: Event: BRICS Energy Ministers' Meeting
Venue: Gurugram, Haryana
Date: June 25–26, 2026
India holds BRICS Presidency in 2026

title: Iran Refuses US Peace Talks Unless Lebanon War Is Stopped
summary: Iran has declared it will not enter into peace negotiations with the United States unless the ongoing war in Lebanon is brought to a halt. This stance follows reported back-channel discussions between the two countries, including a potential nuclear or regional security deal. The statement signals continued geopolitical tensions in West Asia, which have had cascading effects on global oil markets and Indian startup funding.
exam_angle: Relevant for West Asia geopolitics, Iran–US relations, India's energy security (India imports oil from the region), and impact on global markets.
key_facts: Iran refuses US peace talks unless Lebanon war is stopped
Context: ongoing conflict in Lebanon and Iran–US nuclear/security negotiations
West Asia crisis has reduced startup funding in India by 43% YoY since March (Business Standard)
India has strategic energy and diaspora interests in West Asia

title: AIIA Celebrates 12th International Day of Yoga 2026 with Nationwide Events
summary: The All India Institute of Ayurveda (AIIA), under the Ministry of Ayush, celebrated the 12th International Day of Yoga (IDY) 2026 with enthusiastic participation and diverse wellness activities across India. International Day of Yoga is observed annually on June 21, following India's proposal at the United Nations General Assembly in 2014. The theme and events highlighted India's leadership in promoting traditional medicine and holistic wellness globally.
exam_angle: Connects to India's soft power diplomacy through yoga, the role of the Ministry of Ayush, and UN recognition of International Day of Yoga — a recurring current affairs topic.
key_facts: 12th International Day of Yoga: June 21, 2026
Organised by: All India Institute of Ayurveda (AIIA), Ministry of Ayush
IDY adopted by UNGA: December 2014, proposed by PM Narendra Modi
First IDY observed: June 21, 2015

title: West Asia Crisis Pulls Indian Startup Funding Down 43% Year-on-Year Since March 2026
summary: The ongoing conflict in West Asia has contributed to a 43% year-on-year decline in startup funding in India since March 2026, according to Business Standard. The geopolitical uncertainty has dampened investor sentiment, particularly from Middle Eastern sovereign wealth funds and global venture capital firms with exposure to the region. This underscores the linkage between global geopolitical instability and domestic startup ecosystem health.
exam_angle: Links geopolitical risk to domestic economic impact; relevant for GS Paper 3 topics on startup ecosystem, foreign investment, and global economic interdependence.
key_facts: Indian startup funding fell 43% YoY since March 2026
Primary cause attributed: West Asia/Middle East geopolitical crisis
Reflects dependence of Indian startups on global investor sentiment
Middle Eastern SWFs are significant investors in Indian startups

title: Government's Sovereign AI Model Strategy Questioned Amid National AI Policy Debate
summary: A Business Standard analysis raised questions about the Indian government's strategy of developing sovereign AI models, arguing that such models may not be the most effective approach for India's AI ambitions. The debate centres on whether India should build its own large language models (LLMs) or leverage existing global models while focusing resources on AI applications, data governance, and compute infrastructure. This comes amid India's IndiaAI Mission and broader national AI policy discussions.
exam_angle: Relevant for India's AI policy, IndiaAI Mission, digital sovereignty, and debates on technology self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat in tech). GS Paper 3 Sci-Tech topic.
key_facts: Debate: India's sovereign AI model strategy vs. leveraging global AI platforms
Related policy: IndiaAI Mission (approved by Cabinet, 2024)
Key questions: AI compute, data governance, and application-layer innovation
India's AI strategy is part of broader Digital India and Atmanirbhar Bharat frameworks

title: NEET Re-Examination: Over 2 Million Indian Medical Students Resit Exam After Alleged Paper Leak
summary: More than 2 million Indian students were required to resit the NEET (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test) medical entrance examination following allegations of a paper leak. The controversy has reignited debate over the integrity of national-level competitive examinations, the role of the National Testing Agency (NTA), and calls for exam reform. The Supreme Court and government have been under pressure to ensure fair conduct of the examination.
exam_angle: Connects to NTA reforms, right to fair examination (Article 21), governance of centrally administered entrance tests, and federalism debates (states vs. Centre control over medical admissions).
key_facts: Over 2 million students resit NEET following alleged paper leak
Examining body: National Testing Agency (NTA), under Ministry of Education
NEET is the single national entrance test for MBBS/BDS admissions
Controversy has prompted calls for NTA restructuring and exam security reforms

title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Leaves Google DeepMind for Anthropic
summary: John Jumper, who won the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for his work on AlphaFold (AI-based protein structure prediction), has left Google DeepMind to join Anthropic, a leading AI safety company. This high-profile move signals continued competition for top AI talent globally. AlphaFold, developed at DeepMind, revolutionised structural biology and drug discovery.
exam_angle: Relevant for Sci-Tech section: AlphaFold's significance in biology and medicine, Nobel Prize 2024 in Chemistry, and global AI talent competition.
key_facts: John Jumper: Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024 (for AlphaFold protein structure prediction)
Moves from Google DeepMind to Anthropic
AlphaFold: AI model that predicts 3D protein structures, transforming biology and drug discovery
Anthropic: AI safety-focused company, known for Claude AI model

title: US President Trump Removes Anthropic from National Security Threat Designation
summary: US President Donald Trump reportedly no longer views Anthropic, a leading artificial intelligence company, as a national security threat, reversing an earlier stance. Anthropic, founded by former OpenAI researchers, develops the Claude family of AI models and focuses on AI safety research. The policy shift may ease regulatory constraints and facilitate broader US government collaboration with Anthropic on AI projects.
exam_angle: Relevant for understanding US AI policy, the geopolitics of AI regulation, and the role of AI safety companies in national security frameworks — a Sci-Tech/IR crossover topic.
key_facts: Anthropic: AI safety company, develops Claude LLM; founded by former OpenAI researchers
Trump administration removes Anthropic's national security threat designation
Signals potential US government partnership with Anthropic on AI
Relevant context: US–China AI competition and regulation of advanced AI models
